summary: Embeds SQL migration files and applies them in filename-sorted order using a migrations tracking table

requires: migrations/*.sql files embedded at compile time via go:embed
database connection is open and writable
guarantees: Each migration is applied exactly once — tracked by filename in the migrations table
Each migration executes inside a transaction; failure rolls back and propagates the error
Migrations run in lexicographic filename order (001_, 002_, ...)

effects: DDL and DML execution on the SQLite database

observations.text: Uses go:embed to bundle SQL files into the binary — no runtime filesystem dependency on migration files after compilation.

Migration idempotency relies on filename-keyed rows in the migrations table (created by migration 001 itself). On a fresh DB, createMigrationsTable creates this table before any migration runs, so migration 001's CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for migrations is a no-op.
Migration files are sorted lexicographically by filename; a file named '10_...' would sort before '002_...' if prefixes ever exceed single digits. The current naming (001–004) is safe.
Once applied, a migration is never re-run even if its SQL content changes on disk or in the binary — only the filename key is checked.
